subsections.name: Anecdotes about Other Groups of People
Jokes about Clergymen and Religious Figures
Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
Supernatural or Enchanted Wife/Husband or Other Relative

tales.title: The Theft of Fish
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Fox plays dead on a road, fisherman tosses him on the cart, fox throws fish off.  Subsumes former AT 1*; frequently combined with type 2.

tales.title: The Tail-Fisher
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Why the Bear Is Stumpy-Tailed (Asbjørnsen & Moe)
Fox and Lapp (Andrew Lang Brown Fairy Book)
tales.notes: Fox persuades bear/wolf to fish with its tail through ice; tail freezes off.  Native American 'How the Bear Lost His Tail' is a parallel.

tales.title: Sham Blood (Sham-Sick Trickster)
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Fox/jackal fakes wounds to ride on the back of a stronger animal; pairs with type 4.

tales.title: Carrying the Sham-Sick Trickster
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Pair to type 3; the dupe carries the trickster who has feigned weakness.

tales.title: Biting the Foot
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Wolf bites a frozen tree root, mistaking it for the fox's foot.

tales.title: Animal Captor Persuaded to Talk
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Liar Mvkang and the Water Snake (Burmese)
tales.notes: Captive prey escapes by tricking captor into opening mouth/speaking. Related to Chaucer's Nun's Priest's Tale.

tales.title: The Calling of Names (Three Tree Names)
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Bear and the Fox Who Made a Bet (Norwegian)

tales.title: The Painting (False Beauty Treatment)
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Title attested in some open-source listings but not verified in Missouri or Ashliman tables. Treat as provisional.

tales.title: The Unjust Partner
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Uther absorbed former AT 9B into ATU 9. Missouri table notes 'formerly AT 9B'.

tales.title: The Theft of Butter (Honey) by Playing Godfather
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Cat and Mouse in Partnership (Grimm KHM 2)
Mister Rabbit Nibbles Up the Butter  (Joel Chandler Harris, Uncle Remus)
tales.notes: One of the most widespread fox tales; Reynard cycle and Uncle Remus parallels.

tales.title: Eating His Own Entrails
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Title attested in Uther but no exemplar tale could be verified in open-web English-language indices.

tales.title: The Wolf Descends into the Well in One Bucket and Rescues the Fox in the Other
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Fox and Wolf (Andrew Lang Orange Fairy Book)
The Wolf and the Fox in the Well (French)
Fox and Rabbit in the Well (African-American)
Roman de Renart branch IV (Renart's well episode)
tales.notes: Famous Reynard cycle episode.

tales.title: The Wolf Plays Dead
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Title attested in standard listings; older AT 33* 'Father of a Hundred Tricks' and AT 33** 'Fox in Inishkea' were absorbed/reassigned near ATU 41 area.

tales.title: The Wolf Dives into the Water for Reflected Cheese
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Wolf, the Laborer, the Fox, and the Cheese (British)
The Wolf and the Fox in the Well (French)
tales.notes: Closely related to ATU 32 and ATU 92 (lion's reflection).

tales.title: The Fox in Disguise Violates the She-Bear
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Uther title sometimes rendered 'The Fox Disguised as a Doctor Tricks the She-Bear'.

tales.title: The Fox as Nursemaid for the Bear
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Little Boy-Bear Nurses the Alligator Children (African-American)
The Fox as Herdsman (Asbjørnsen & Moe, listed as 37*)

tales.title: Claw in Split Tree
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Title attested in Uther 2004 but no specific exemplar verified in open-web English-language indices. Standard plot: wolf/bear caught with paw in cleft tree after wedge is removed.

tales.title: The Wolf Overeats in the Cellar
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: The Wolf and the Fox (Grimm KHM 73)
Struggle Between the Wolf and the Fox (German)
tales.notes: Often paired with Grimm KHM 73;  absorbed older AT 33* and 33** material.

tales.title: The Bear and the Honey
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)

tales.title: The Sick Lion
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: The Lion, the Wolf, and the Fox (Aesop, Perry 258)
The Lion, Wolf, and Fox (La Fontaine)
Liver of the Wise and Liver of the Foolish (Egyptian)
The King of the Tigers Is Sick (Malay)
tales.notes: Classic ailing-lion fable; the fox tricks the wolf into being flayed  for medicinal hide.

tales.title: The Lion's Share
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: La Génisse, la Chèvre et la Brebis (La Fontaine I.6)
tales.notes: Thompson motif J811.1.  Historical 51*** subtype 'Fox as umpire to divide cheese'.

tales.title: The Donkey without a Heart
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: The Heart and Ears of the Buried Ass (Aesop, Perry 336)
The Lion, the Jackal, and the Donkey (Panchatantra)
tales.notes: Motif K402.3.  Fox/jackal eats heart and ears, tells lion the ass had none.

tales.title: The Fox at Court (Reynard at Court)
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Motif J864.2. Fox condemned to hang asks to see the geese first.

tales.title: The Animals Build a Road
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Various 'lazy fox refuses to dig well' tales (African-American Brer Rabbit
tales.notes: Motif A2233.1. Animals refuse to help dig well/make road and are punished.

tales.title: The Fox and the Magpie (Fox Through Sleight Steals the Young Magpies)
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Motif K1788. Fox threatens to climb/cut down tree; bird feeds him her young.

tales.title: The Raven with Cheese in His Mouth (Fox and Crow)
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Le Corbeau et le Renard (La Fontaine I.2)
tales.notes: Motif K334.1.  One of the most internationally widespread Aesopic fables.

tales.title: The Fox and the Sour Grapes
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: The Fox and the Grapes (Aesop, Perry 15)
Le Renard et les Raisins (La Fontaine III.11)
tales.notes: Motif J871.  Source of the idiom 'sour grapes'.

tales.title: The Fox and the Crane Invite Each Other
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: The Fox and the Stork (Aesop/Phaedrus 1.26, Perry 426)
Le Renard et la Cigogne (La Fontaine I.18)
tales.notes: Motif J1565.1. Featured in Bruegel's Netherlandish Proverbs (1559).

tales.title: The Fox Persuades the Cock to Crow with Closed Eyes
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: The Fox and the Cock (Aesop, Perry 671)
Chaucer's Nun's Priest's Tale (Chanticleer and the Fox)
tales.notes: Motif K721. Often combined with reverse-trick where cock then asks fox to bless meal.

tales.title: Peace Among the Animals
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: The Cock and the Fox (Aesop, Perry 671 variants)
tales.notes: Motif J1421. Fox claims a universal peace; flees when dogs approach.

tales.title: The Fox Rids Himself of Fleas
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Various Norwegian, German, Danish, Scottish, Indian/Pakistani (jackal), and African-American (Georgia) variants
tales.notes: Motif K921. Fox submerges into water leaving only mouthful of wool above; fleas migrate; fox spits out wool.

tales.title: The She-Fox's Suitors
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Die Hochzeit der Frau Füchsin / The Wedding of Mrs. Fox (Grimm KHM 38, both versions)
tales.notes: Motif N681. Grimm KHM 38 also classified as types 1350, 1352*, and 1510. Nine-tailed fox feigns death to test wife.

tales.title: The Fox Caught by the Butcher
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Irish tradition (15 catalogued variants in Duchas)
tales.notes: Motif K634.1. Escape by throwing captor's clothes on the fire.

tales.title: Fox in the Swollen River
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Motif J873. Drowning fox claims he is merely swimming to a distant town.

tales.title: The Jackal Trapped in the Animal Hide
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
exemplars: Panchatantra and Hitopadesha (Indian) — jackal eats into elephant carcass and is trapped when hide dries
tales.notes: Motifs K1022.1.1 and K565.2.

tales.title: Fox Jeers at the Fox Trap
tales.subsection: Wild Animals: The Clever Fox (Other Animal)
tales.notes: Motif J655.2.

tales.title: More Cowardly Than the Hare
exemplars: The Hares and the Frogs (Aesop, Perry 138)
Le Lièvre et les Grenouilles (La Fontaine II.14)
tales.notes: Motif J881.1. Hare finds creatures more timid than himself and laughs till his lip splits.

tales.title: Contest of Frost and the Hare
tales.notes: Motif H1541.1. Contest in enduring cold.

tales.title: The Hare Instructs His Sons
tales.notes: Motif J61. Hare instructs sons to use their eyes to advantage.

tales.title: The Rabbit Rides Fox A-Courting
exemplars: Mr. Rabbit Grossly Deceives Mr. Fox (Joel Chandler Harris, Uncle Remus)
tales.notes: Motif K1241. Trickster rides dupe horseback.

tales.title: Blinding the Guard
tales.notes: Motif K621. Escape by blinding the guard.

tales.title: The Help of the Weak
exemplars: The Lion and the Mouse (Aesop, Perry 150)
Le Lion et le Rat (La Fontaine II.11)
tales.notes: Motif B371.1. Mouse gnaws net and liberates captured lion.

tales.title: The Wolf and the Crane
exemplars: The Wolf and the Crane (Aesop, Perry 156)
Le Loup et la Cigogne (La Fontaine III.9)
tales.notes: Motif W154.3. Crane removes bone from wolf's throat; wolf refuses payment.

tales.title: The Stag Admires Himself in a Spring
exemplars: The Stag at the Pool (Aesop, Perry 74)
Le Cerf se voyant dans l'eau (La Fontaine VI.9)
tales.notes: Motif L461. Stag scorns his legs but is proud of horns; antlers cause his death.

tales.title: The Animal Holds Tightly to His Supposed Protector
tales.notes: Motif K713.1.2. Animal allows himself to be tied to another for safety.

tales.title: Too Cold for Hare to Build House in Winter
exemplars: Irish folktales (5 stories catalogued in Duchas)
tales.notes: Hare laments cold in winter, decides to build a house, but in summer forgets and goes without.

tales.title: The Mouse, the Bird, and the Sausage
exemplars: Von dem Mäuschen, Vögelchen und der Bratwurst (Grimm KHM 23)
tales.notes: Motif J512.7. Three companions exchange roles with disastrous results.

tales.title: The Heart of a Monkey as Medicine
exemplars: Indian Jataka tales (sea creature seeks monkey's heart)
tales.notes: Motif K544. Escape by alleged possession of external soul; monkey claims heart is left in tree.

tales.title: The Lion Dives for His Reflection
exemplars: The Lion and the Hare / Bhâsuraka (Panchatantra)
Singh Rajah and the Cunning Little Jackals (Mary Frere, Old Deccan Days, 1868)
The Hare and the Lions (Tibetan, W. F. O'Connor)
Brother Rabbit Conquers Brother Lion (Joel Chandler Harris)
tales.notes: Motif K1715.1. Weak animal shows strong his own reflection and frightens him.

tales.title: The Master Taken Seriously
exemplars: L'Alouette et ses petits avec le maître d'un champ (La Fontaine IV.22)
tales.notes: Motif J1031. Grain will only be cut when farmer attends to it himself.

tales.title: The Wolf Is Caught Because of His Singing
exemplars: 'Wolf, Guest of the Dog, Sings' (AT 100 variants)
tales.notes: Motif J581.1. Wolf as dog's guest sings and is discovered. Survives unchanged from AT to ATU.

tales.title: The Old Dog as Rescuer of the Child
exemplars: Old Sultan / Der alte Sultan (Grimm KHM 48)
The Bear, the Dog, and the Cat (Afanasyev, Russian)
tales.notes: Motif K231.1.3.

tales.title: The Dog as the Wolf's Shoemaker
exemplars: 'Fox Makes Clothes for the Wolf' (AT 102 variants)
tales.notes: Motif K254.1.

tales.title: War Between Wild Animals and Domestic Animals
exemplars: Struggle Between the Wolf and the Fox (German, Kurt)
tales.notes: Motif B262. Former AT 104 'War Between the Village Animals and the Forest Animals' merged into ATU 103 by Uther 2004.

tales.title: Hedgehog's Only Trick
tales.notes: Motif K522.

tales.title: The Cat's Only Trick
exemplars: The Fox and the Cat / Der Fuchs und die Katze (Grimm KHM 75)
tales.notes: Motif J1662. Cat with one trick (climbing tree) escapes; fox with hundred tricks is caught.

tales.title: Belling the Cat
tales.notes: Motif J671.1. Source of idiom 'to bell the cat'. Often confused with the distinct fable 'The Cat and the Mice' (Perry 79).

tales.title: The Cat and the Mouse Converse
exemplars: Catties Sit in the Kiln-Ring Spinning (Scottish)
tales.notes: Motif K561.1.1.

tales.title: Country Mouse Visits the Town Mouse
exemplars: Le rat de ville et le rat des champs (La Fontaine I.9)
The Tale of Johnny Town-Mouse (Beatrix Potter, 1918)
tales.notes: Motif J211.2.

tales.title: The First to See the Sunrise
exemplars: 'Wager to See the Sunrise First' (AT 120 variants)
tales.notes: Motif K52.

tales.title: Wolves Climb on Top of One Another to a Tree
tales.notes: Motif J2133.6.

tales.title: The Wolf Loses His Prey (Animal Loses Prey by False Plea)
tales.notes: Motif K550. Parent type for the 122A–122Z subtype cluster.

tales.title: The Wolf and the Kids
exemplars: Der Wolf und die sieben jungen Geißlein / The Wolf and the Seven Young Goats (Grimm KHM 5)
The She-Goat, the Kid, and the Wolf (Aesop)
Marie de France 'The Wolf, the Goat, and the Kid'
The Wolf, the Goat and the Kid (La Fontaine IV)
The Nanny Goat with the Iron Horns (El-Shamy, Egyptian)
tales.notes: Motifs K1832, F913, Q426. Wolf disguises voice/paw to gain entry; kids hide; rescue from wolf's belly.

tales.title: Blowing the House In (Three Little Pigs)
exemplars: The Story of the Three Little Pigs (Halliwell-Phillipps, Nursery Rhymes of England, c.1843/1886
The Fox and the Pixies (Dartmoor, 1853 — earliest known printed version)
The Awful Fate of Mr. Wolf and The Story of the Pigs (Joel Chandler Harris)
tales.notes: Motif Z81.

tales.title: The Wolf Flees from the Wolf-Head
exemplars: Ram, the Cat, the Twelve Wolves (Afanasyev, Russian)
tales.notes: Motif K1715.3.

tales.title: The Sheep Chases the Wolf
tales.notes: Motif K1715.

tales.title: The Animals in Night Quarters (Bremen Town Musicians)
exemplars: Die Bremer Stadtmusikanten (Grimm KHM 27, 1819, from Dorothea Viehmann)
The Sheep and the Pig Who Set Up House (Asbjørnsen, Norwegian)
The Story of the White Pet (Campbell, Scottish)
How the Animals Kept the Lions Away (Arab)
tales.notes: Motifs B296, K335, K1161. Closely related to ATU 210. Aarne suggested Asian origin; Delarue identified Western (domestic animals) and Eastern (small animals) variants.

tales.title: Tiger as False Friend to the Cow
tales.notes: Motif J427.

tales.title: Goat Admires His Horns in the Water
tales.notes: Motif K1775.

tales.title: Wolf Surprises Pig in Apple Tree
tales.notes: Motif K1043.

tales.title: The Filthy Hog and the Clean Fish
The Three Teachings of the Bird
tales.notes: Captured bird gains freedom by giving three pieces of advice (e.g. don't regret the past).

tales.title: Music Lessons for Wild Animals
tales.notes: Man tricks wolf/bear into a trap by pretending to teach it to play a fiddle.

tales.title: The Man as the Shepherd of the Wild Animals
tales.notes: Man hires animal to guard flock; animal eats them. Related to type 37.

tales.title: The Gelding of the Bear and the Fetching of the Honey
exemplars: The Bear and the Honey (Russian/Finnish variants)
tales.notes: Trickster (often man) castrates bear or leads it into a trap under the guise of finding honey.

tales.title: Bear-Food (Man Tells Bear of a Better Food)
exemplars: The Bear and the Man (Grimm variants)
tales.notes: Man escapes bear by promising to show it "better food" (often involves a trap).

tales.title: Ingratitude Is the World's Reward
exemplars: The Tiger the Brahmin and the Jackal (India)
tales.notes: Rescued animal tries to eat its rescuer; judge (fox) tricks it back into the trap.

tales.title: Androcles and the Lion
tales.notes: Man pulls thorn from lion's paw; lion later spares him in the arena.

tales.title: Learning to Fear Men
exemplars: The Wolf and the Man (Grimm KHM 72)
The Lion Searches for Man (La Fontaine)
tales.notes: Animal seeks the strongest creature; man shoots it teaching it fear.

tales.title: The Wild Animal on the Sleigh
tales.notes: Animal boards man's sleigh and is eventually thrown off or trapped.

tales.title: The Trusty Lion
exemplars: The Lion and the Mouse (Aesop variants)
tales.notes: Focuses on the lion as a domestic protector or faithful companion.

tales.title: Grateful Animals; Ungrateful Man
tales.notes: Man rescues several animals and a human; only animals help him later.

tales.title: The Bear and the Honey-Waggon
tales.notes: Variant of honey-trap motifs.

tales.title: The Bear and the Fisherman
tales.notes: Man tricks bear while fishing.

tales.title: The Wolf and the Shepherds
tales.notes: Shepherds outwit or catch the wolf.

tales.title: The Priest's Wolf
tales.notes: Wolf interacts with a clergyman (often comedic).

tales.title: The Dog and the Wolf
tales.notes: Conflict or partnership between a wild wolf and a domestic dog in a human context.

tales.title: The Preparation of the Wolf for the Journey
tales.notes: Comedic dressing or preparation of a wolf.

tales.title: The Bear and the Bee-Hives
tales.notes: Bear stung while raiding hives.

tales.title: The Musician and the Wolf
tales.notes: Musical interaction similar to 151.

tales.title: The Man and the Bear in the Sleigh
tales.notes: Variation of sleigh-boarding motifs.

tales.title: The Wolf as the Man's Partner
tales.notes: Wolf and man attempt to cooperate (usually failing).

tales.title: The Three Bears
tales.notes: Intruder tries beds/porridge of bears. Originally an old woman later Goldilocks.

tales.title: The Tarbaby and the Rabbit
tales.notes: Trickster gets stuck to a sticky figure. Widespread in Africa and the Americas.

tales.title: The Man and the Snake
tales.notes: Moralistic interactions between a man and a serpent.

tales.title: The Thief and the Tiger
tales.notes: Tiger mistakes thief for a more dangerous creature.

tales.title: The Bear and the Honey-Barrel
tales.notes: Bear trapped by its head in a barrel.

tales.title: The Giant and the Tailor
exemplars: The Giant and the Tailor (Grimm KHM 183)
tales.notes: Small man outwits large beast/giant.

tales.title: The Certificate for the Dog and the Cat
exemplars: The Dog the Cat and the Mouse (various European)
tales.notes: Explains why dogs/cats are enemies (often a lost legal document).

tales.title: The Dog's House
tales.notes: Dog decides to build house in winter (too cold) but forgets in summer.

tales.title: The Two Dogs
tales.notes: Contrast between well-fed pet and hungry stray.

tales.title: The Sheep the Duck and the Cock
exemplars: The Town Musicians of Bremen (Grimm variants)
tales.notes: Related to 130; animals seek a new life.

tales.title: Sheep Duck and Cock in Peril at Sea
tales.notes: Animals take a boat trip; peril ensues.

tales.title: The Cat and the Dog as Travelers
tales.notes: Similar to 204 but terrestrial.

tales.title: The Animals Build a Road
tales.notes: Cooperative effort among farm animals.

tales.title: The Ass and the Horse
tales.notes: Horse refuses to help carry load until ass dies.

tales.title: The Foolish Cat
tales.notes: Cat tricked by prey or another pet.

tales.title: The Dog in the Manger
tales.notes: Animal prevents others from eating what it cannot.

tales.title: The Traveling Animals and the Wicked Man
tales.notes: Animals/objects hide in man's house and attack him when he returns.

tales.title: The Hog Who Was So Tired of His Daily Food
tales.notes: Complaining animal realizes status quo is better.

tales.title: The Lying Goat
tales.notes: Goat tells owner it hasn't eaten; sons are banished; owner finds out goat lied.

tales.title: The Goat and the Wolf
tales.notes: Goat outwits wolf.

tales.title: The Ass in the Lion's Skin
exemplars: The Ass in the Lion's Skin (Aesop)
tales.notes: Donkey scares others with skin until its voice/ears betray it.

tales.title: The Dog and the Wolf as Partners
tales.notes: Domestic/Wild animal cooperation.

tales.title: The Ass as the Partner of the Wolf
tales.notes: Wolf uses donkey as a dupe.

tales.title: The Cat as the Servant of the Priest
tales.notes: Pet-cleric interaction.

tales.title: The Wolf as the Dog's Guest
tales.notes: Wild animal visiting the farm.

tales.title: The Council of the Birds
exemplars: The King of the Birds (Grimm KHM 171)
tales.notes: Birds decide to elect a king (often by flight height).

tales.title: The Election of the King of the Birds
tales.notes: Wren hides on eagle's back to fly highest.

tales.title: The War Between the Birds and the Beasts
exemplars: The Willow-Wren and the Bear (Grimm KHM 102)
tales.notes: Gnat helps birds win by stinging fox/general.

tales.title: The Lark and the Wolf
tales.notes: Wolf outwitted by small bird.

tales.title: The Wedding of the Birds
tales.notes: Fable regarding bird behavior.

tales.title: The Crane and the Fox Invite Each Other
tales.notes: Same as 60; often cross-listed in Other Animals.

tales.title: The Nightingale and the Blindworm
exemplars: The Nightingale and the Blindworm (Grimm KHM 171a)
tales.notes: Explains why nightingale has two eyes and blindworm none.

tales.title: The Geese's Eternal Prayer
exemplars: The Fox and the Geese (Grimm KHM 86)
tales.notes: Geese persuade fox to let them pray first; they pray forever ("ga-ga").

tales.title: The Titmouse and the Bear
tales.notes: Small bird tricks the bear.

tales.title: The Magpie's Nest
tales.notes: Other birds leave magpie's lesson early; that's why their nests are poor.

tales.title: The Heron and the Fish
tales.notes: Heron attempts to trick fish into "safety."

tales.title: The Goose That Laid the Golden Eggs
exemplars: The Goose with the Golden Eggs (Aesop)
tales.notes: Owner kills bird to get all gold at once; finds nothing.

tales.title: The Crow and the Pitcher
tales.notes: Crow drops stones into water to raise level and drink.

tales.title: The Nightingale and the Blindworm (Reclassification)
tales.notes: Sometimes cross-listed with 226.

tales.title: The Bird and the Hunter
tales.notes: Hunter outwitted by bird's cleverness.

tales.title: Imitating Bird Sounds
exemplars: The Crumbs on the Table (Grimm KHM 190)
tales.notes: Nonsense bird-rhyme type.

tales.title: The Parrot and the Cat
tales.notes: Parrot tricks or mimics other pets.

tales.title: The Owl and the Birds
tales.notes: Owl gives advice birds ignore.

tales.title: The Fox and the Crane (Cross-ref)
tales.notes: Duplicate of 60 in some bird indices.

tales.title: The Lion and the Hare
tales.notes: Rabbit/Hare outwits lion.

tales.title: The Crane and the Heron
tales.notes: Two birds fail to agree on a proposal.

tales.title: The Crow and the Peacock
tales.notes: Peacock mocks crow until its own beauty attracts a hunter.

tales.title: The Parrot and His Master
tales.notes: Master tests parrot's truthfulness.

tales.title: The Raven in Borrowed Feathers
tales.notes: Bird tries to win beauty contest with others' feathers; exposed.

tales.title: The Swallow and the Other Birds
tales.notes: Birds fail to heed swallow's warning about flax.

tales.title: The Hunter and the Bird (Warning)
tales.notes: Bird teaches man three truths.

tales.title: Each Mother Likes Her Own Children Best
tales.notes: Owl describes children as beautiful; eagle eats them because they are "ugly."

tales.title: The Dog and the Sparrow
exemplars: The Dog and the Sparrow (Grimm KHM 58)
tales.notes: Sparrow takes revenge on carter who killed his dog friend.

tales.title: The Toad and the Frog
tales.notes: Contrast between the two amphibians.

tales.title: The Race Between Animals
tales.notes: Slow animal wins race through persistence or trickery.

tales.title: The Crab and His Mother
tales.notes: Mother tells child to walk straight; child asks her to show how.

tales.title: The Frogs Who Desired a King
exemplars: The Frogs Who Desired a King (Aesop)
tales.notes: Frogs ask for a king; Zeus sends a log then a stork that eats them.

tales.title: The Mouse and the Frog
